free_text: Scattering of Alpha Particles by Oxygen.II. Bombarding
Energy Range 10 to 19 MeV
Alpha-particle beam (5-12 nA) of continuously variable
energy.
SD. In Subent 006 values in DATA section
without heading were deleted. Duplication in
energy (or angle) values in Subents 2-7 were checked
and corrected. Cosmetic changes in all Subents.
OS. Corrections in sub.8
Oxygen targets prepared by vacuum deposition of thin
layers of lithium metal onto thin carbon backings with
subsequent oxidation of the lithium to form LiOH.
Beam collected in a well-insulated Faraday cup.
Unresolved yield due to contaminants such as nitrogen
and fluorine, estimated by comparing back-angle pulse-
height distributions with those for thin quartz (SiO2)
targets. No impurities with mass number ~16 detected.
Eight p-type silicon-junction detectors
operated simultaneously.
Each detector had a resolution on the order of 150 keV.
90-degree analyzing magnet calibrated using threshold
measurement of 13C(p,n)13N reaction with an assumed
threshold energy of 3.237 MeV.
Estimated error 12%, except where cross
section is <5 mb/sr, low counting rates resulted in
large statistical errors.
Digitizing error in energy.
Digitizing error in data.
Maximum calibration error not greater than +-20 KeV.
Scanned from Figs.1,2 of PR,160,791,1967 at
RFNC-VNIIEF.
SD. Duplication in energy values corrected.

layers of lithium metal onto thin carbon backings with

at RFNC-VNIIEF.
SD. Duplication in angle values corrected.
Lead oxide target deposited on gold disks.
3-in.x 3-in. NaI(Tl) crystal with photo-
multiplier placed 2 in. from target.
Peak-to-total ratio and efficiency of crystal for
detection of 6-MeV gamma rays estimated by
extrapolating known curves for the NaI(Tl) crystal.
R.L.Heath, Report, IDO-16408 (1957).
Target thickness determined by measuring the elastic-
scattering yield from the same target.
Estimated error in absolute cross section.
Digitizing error in energy.

SD. Duplication in energy values corrected.
Lead oxide target deposited on gold disks, and
mounted in a thin walled chamber. Target thickness
about 25 keV for 10 MeV alphas.
3-in.x 3-in. NaI(Tl) crystal, mounted on a
rotating arm at a distance of 10-in. from axis of
rotation.
Another 3-in. x 3-in. NaI(Tl) crystal was placed at
fixed angle of 90.degr. with respect to the beam and
served as a monitor.
Peak-to-total ratio and efficiency of crystal for
detection of 6-MeV gamma rays estimated by
extrapolating known curves for the NaI(Tl) crystal.
R.L.Heath, Report, IDO-16408 (1957).
NaI(Tl) crystal placed at fixed angle of 90 degrees
with respect to incident beam served as a monitor.
Target thickness determined by measuring the elastic-
scattering yield from the same target.
Data at 15.705 MeV resonance corrected for
contributions from other levels by subtracting yield
just off resonance.
Estimated error in absolute cross section.
Digitizing error in data
